question: Explain the trend in thermal stability of Group 2 carbonates.
full_answer: Down the group the M2+ ion has lower charge density. So it polarises the carbonate ion less. The carbonate ion is less distorted and is less likely to decompose.

question: Explain the trend in solubility of the Group 2 sulfates.
full_answer: The solubility of the Group 2 sulfates decreases down the group because both lattice enthalpy and hydration enthalpy become less exothermic. The hydration enthalpy decreases by a greater amount, so dissolution becomes less favourable.

rounds.prompt: The solubility of the Group 2 sulfates _____ down the group because both lattice enthalpy and hydration enthalpy become less exothermic. The hydration enthalpy decreases by a greater amount, so dissolution becomes less favourable.

The solubility of the Group 2 sulfates decreases down the group because both lattice enthalpy and hydration enthalpy become less exothermic. The hydration enthalpy decreases by a greater amount, so _____ becomes less favourable.

question: Explain why the solution formed when magnesium nitride reacts with water has pH greater than 7.
full_answer: Magnesium nitride reacts with water to form ammonia and magnesium hydroxide. Ammonia reacts with water to produce hydroxide ions, so the solution is alkaline.
